steps.title: Check current langflow setup
steps.description:
Verify your langflow version and configuration:
```bash
pip show langflow | grep Version
```
This feature requires a working langflow installation.

steps.title: Review the feature request
steps.description: LM Studio (https://lmstudio.ai/) is a local LLM inference tool. This feature adds a native LMStudio model component to Langflow, enabling users to connect to a locally running LM Studio server.

steps.title: Apply the LMStudio component
steps.description:
The fix adds a new `LMStudioModel` component that extends the base LCModelComponent with LM Studio-specific inputs:
```python
inputs = LCModelComponent._base_inputs + [
    IntInput(
        name="max_tokens",
        display_name="Max Tokens",
        advanced=True,
        info="The maximum number of tokens to generate. Set to 0 for unlimited tokens.",
        range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=128000),
    ),
    ...
]
```
A CI linting issue (RUF005) was resolved by using iterable unpacking instead of list concatenation.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the LMStudio model component appears in the Langflow UI and can connect to a running LM Studio server.

resolution.summary: Added a native LMStudio model component to Langflow that extends LCModelComponent, enabling users to connect to locally running LM Studio servers for inference.
codeSnippets:
inputs = LCModelComponent._base_inputs + [
    IntInput(
        name="max_tokens",
        display_name="Max Tokens",
        advanced=True,
        info="The maximum number of tokens to generate. Set to 0 for unlimited tokens.",
        range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=128000),
    ),
    ...
]

prerequisites.description: Python 3.10+ with langflow installed (pip install langflow). Optionally Docker for containerized deployment.
